metrics.narrative: Moore articulated quantified business scope and operational pain on the 21 May 2026 discovery call. CPO Koen Bergs stated controllable spend of approximately €80M annually across 5,000+ suppliers and 72 locations, with only 120 trained buyers serving 2,300 employees. Arthur Depoorter noted the imbalance: 'In Belgium, there was a community of 2,300 companions, with only 120 trained for purchases, while everyone could submit expenses, highlighting an imbalance.' Pain points include AFAS's 'old-fashioned procurement tooling that fails to support established processes' (Koen, 21 May), manual classification overhead ('what is an expense and what is a purchase' – Koen, 21 May), and change management burden ('ensuring all foundational elements are in place has become a significant challenge' – Guus, 21 May). No formal ROI model or savings target quantified yet, but the stated spend volume, supplier count, and process fragmentation provide a clear business case baseline. Next move is to map Zycus features against Moore's finance and procurement capability map (requested by Guus on the call) to quantify coverage and savings potential.

competition.narrative: Competition is limited and clearly defined. (1) AFAS is the incumbent procurement and finance system being replaced. Koen Bergs stated on 21 May 2026: 'We work today with Afas in a very old fashioned way… the tool is not helping… to fulfill that process. So that's why we are looking for a new finance ERP' and 'Frustration with AFAS's old-fashioned procurement tooling that fails to support established processes' (pain point note). AFAS is confirmed as the incumbent being displaced; no renewal consideration, so threat level is dormant (legacy inertia only). (2) No other procurement suite competitors named on the discovery call or in Salesforce fields (Competitors__c is unknown/not populated). (3) Moore is running a parallel finance ERP selection ('now deciding what kind of finance ERP we will do', Koen 21 May), which may introduce an indirect competitive dynamic if a finance ERP vendor (e.g. SAP, Oracle, Workday) bundles procurement and Moore consolidates to a single platform. (4) Contractify is the current contract management tool used by legal, which Koen described as 'a very good tool, but it's too complex to put everything in, and we have no data owner. So… if you use it once a year and you have to put in a contract, yeah, it's too complex' (21 May transcript). Contractify is a point solution for legal contracts and independent contractor agreements, not a full procurement competitor, but it occupies the contract management slot and may create adoption inertia. Status: Contractify is a niche incumbent (contract mgmt only), not a full S2P rival, so threat level low. (5) Caroline (Zycus) mentioned on the call that she 'was at Coupa' in her prior role, and Moore may be aware of Coupa as an alternative, but no Coupa evaluation or mention by the buyer. How we win: (1) Unified expense + procurement (Moore's top criterion; Zycus covers it via partnerships with Webexpenses and Numensia for virtual cards, whereas AFAS does not). (2) Intuitive Teams/Slack interface with zero training (Merlin intake) for Moore's 2300 decentralized, untrained users – a massive adoption advantage vs AFAS and traditional ERPs. (3) Multi-language (English/French/Dutch) native support for Belgian market. (4) AI-driven touchless invoicing and dynamic OCR (buyer expressed interest on 21 May call). (5) EU data residency (buyer requirement). (6) Modular/composable (Moore can start with P2P + contract mgmt and add sourcing later, vs monolithic ERP). The wedge is adoption simplicity (Merlin intake in Teams) and expense + procurement unification for a decentralized, multi-language user base that AFAS and traditional finance ERPs cannot serve.

identify_pain.narrative: Moore articulated specific operational and financial pain on the 21 May 2026 discovery call, owned by CPO Koen Bergs. (1) AFAS inadequacy: 'Frustration with AFAS's old-fashioned procurement tooling that fails to support established processes' (pain point note, Koen, 21 May). Koen elaborated: 'We have a clear process, but the tool is not helping… to fulfill that process. So that's why we are looking for a new finance ERP' (21 May transcript). (2) Supplier sprawl and lack of control: 'We have more than 5,000 suppliers, which is big for a company which is in advisory and accountancy' (Koen, 21 May transcript). (3) Expense vs purchase classification burden: 'We mix always expenses with purchase. So that's a big issue today, what is an expense and what is a purchase' (Koen, 21 May transcript). (4) Decentralized, untrained buying population: 'We are really decentralized. We have today 5 key centers… 72 locations in Belgium… 2,300 companions, and… roughly 120 are trained and nominated to do purchases. But there is no such system for expenses. Everybody can do an expense. So that is still an imbalance today' (Koen, 21 May transcript). (5) Change management overhead: 'Ensuring all foundational elements are in place has become a significant challenge in change management' (Guus, 21 May pain point note). (6) Invoice matching and data accuracy: 'The main issue is not the automation of processes but rather the lack of sufficient information, such as accurate purchase order details on invoices, which hinders correct matching' (Guus, 21 May pain point note). Pain is confirmed, quantified (5000+ suppliers, €80M spend, 2300 users, 120 trained buyers), and owned by the CPO.

decision_criteria.narrative: Moore's evaluation criteria were explicitly stated on the 21 May 2026 discovery call and are well documented. (1) Unified expense and procurement management: 'combines expenses and procurements because that's the way this company works, and we are all entrepreneurs… nobody wants to decide if this is an expense or not or a purchase, so we should help them' (Koen, 21 May). (2) Multi-language support (English, French, Dutch) for full Belgian coverage: 'if we would like to have that in 3 languages, English, French, and Dutch, is that possible?' (Koen, 21 May) – confirmed as supported. (3) Intuitive Teams/Slack chat interface requiring zero training for occasional users (Zycus implicit requirement note). (4) EU data residency through local hosting in Germany and Ireland (Zycus implicit requirement note). (5) Flexible API connector framework for external data sources and any ERP (Zycus implicit requirement note). (6) AI-driven touchless invoicing and dynamic OCR for variable invoice formats: Jason asked 'about Dynamic Optical Recognition for handling variable invoice formats, signaling interest in AI-driven invoice processing' (21 May). (7) Virtual card and expense payment integration via partnerships (Webexpenses, Numensia) for streamlined payables (Guus, 21 May). (8) Feature coverage mapping against Moore's finance and procurement capability map (Guus action item, 21 May). These are the confirmed decision gates; no formal scorecard or RFP rubric shared yet.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Buyer is AI Curious. Moore expressed clear interest in AI-driven features (touchless invoicing, dynamic OCR, Teams/Slack bot for zero training) on the 21 May 2026 discovery call, but AI is not the primary driver; the primary driver is operational pain (AFAS inadequacy, 5000+ suppliers, expense vs purchase confusion, decentralized untrained users). Jason asked about dynamic OCR (AI excitement signal), and Guus acknowledged touchless invoicing benefits while cautioning on PO data accuracy prerequisites (pragmatic, not resistant). Koen's top requirement is 'policy driven guides lines of guidance' (21 May), which Merlin intake delivers, but he framed it as process control, not AI excitement. AIS fields are not populated in Salesforce, so no official AI excitement score from Zycus's internal assessment. Calls show AI Curious (interested in AI where it solves a stated pain, not AI-first), not AI Hungry (would not buy on AI alone) or AI Resistant (no skepticism or budget pushback on AI features).

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified-stage deal with strong early discovery (CPO/CFO pain mapped, demo scheduled) now facing active S2P competitor Zycus in parallel evaluation as of late June. Multi-threading established but competitive displacement risk has escalated; monitor demo execution and differentiation urgently.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified deal with strong discovery foundation, clear pain and requirements, and upcoming demo milestone. Three high-leverage win-strategy actions: (1) Multi-thread beyond CPO Koen to map and engage the CFO (budget authority) and finance/digital transformation leads before the demo, so the demo audience includes the Economic Buyer and decision influencers, not only the process owner. (2) Resolve the cloud-only vs on-prem/isolation security concern NOW with detailed technical documentation (data sovereignty, EU hosting, compliance certifications, geopolitical risk mitigation) before the demo, so InfoSec objections do not derail the workshop. (3) Formalize the partner (Joel, supply chain consulting) as an advocate and implementation ally by aligning the partner pricing model and demonstrating how Zycus + partner deliver expense + procurement unification and change management (Moore's top two priorities) better than a bundled finance ERP module; activate the partner to sell Zycus internally at Moore and block finance ERP vendor bundling.

competitive_position.summary: Moore is replacing incumbent AFAS (old-fashioned procurement and finance tooling that fails to support processes, per CPO 21 May 2026). AFAS is dormant as a renewal threat (no consideration of staying); inertia and change management are the only residual risks. No other S2P competitors named. Moore is running a parallel finance ERP selection, which may introduce an indirect competitive dynamic if a finance ERP vendor bundles procurement, but no specific ERP finalist named yet. Contractify (current contract management point solution for legal) occupies the contract mgmt slot but is not a full S2P rival; buyer stated it is too complex for procurement use. No Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, Ivalua, or other S2P peer named. Zycus is the only S2P platform in active evaluation as of 26 Jun 2026. Single strongest current threat: inertia / do-nothing (the risk that Moore delays procurement system selection while finalizing the finance ERP choice, or that the finance ERP vendor bundles procurement and Moore consolidates to avoid dual-vendor complexity). Mitigation: accelerate the demo and workshop (planned June), map Zycus features against Moore's capability map to prove coverage, and position the Zycus + finance ERP integration (API flexibility, ERP-agnostic) as superior to a bundled finance-ERP procurement module.

competitors.name: AFAS
competitors.quote: We work today with Afas in a very old fashioned way. So all the things you described, how difficult it is to order something, we noticed that we have a clear process, but the tool is not helping that to fulfill that process. So that's why we are looking for a new finance ERP. Frustration with AFAS's old-fashioned procurement tooling that fails to support established processes.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 21 May 2026, speaker Koen Bergs (CPO)
competitors.how_we_win: AFAS is being replaced, not defended. Win by proving Zycus can unify expense + procurement (AFAS cannot), deliver intuitive Teams/Slack adoption for 2300 decentralized users (AFAS old-fashioned UI), and provide AI-driven touchless invoicing and multi-language support (AFAS lacks). No renewal fight; this is a displacement.

competitors.name: Contractify
competitors.quote: We are not really using it today. We try, but it's too complex. Yeah. It's I think it's a very good tool, contractify, but it's too complex to put everything in, and we have no data owner. So what we see is if if if you use it once a year and you have to put in a contract, yeah, it's too complex.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 21 May 2026, speaker Koen Bergs (CPO)
competitors.how_we_win: Contractify is a legal-only contract management point solution, not a full S2P platform. Buyer stated it is too complex for procurement use and underutilized. Win by positioning Zycus iContract as a simpler, procurement-native contract module within the full S2P suite, with intake via Merlin in Teams (zero training) and integration to eProcurement and eInvoicing. Contractify stays for legal contracts; Zycus owns procurement contracts.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / delay (finance ERP bundling risk)
competitors.quote: We are now deciding what kind of finance ERP we will do, and that's why Guus is checking all the capabilities because we strongly are aware that there is no good finance tool that is also a good procurement tool. So it will be a combination.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 21 May 2026, speaker Koen Bergs (CPO)
competitors.how_we_win: Moore is running parallel finance ERP and procurement system selections. Risk: the finance ERP vendor (SAP, Oracle, Workday, etc.) bundles procurement and Moore consolidates to a single platform to avoid dual-vendor complexity, or Moore delays procurement decision until finance ERP is chosen. Mitigation: (1) Prove Zycus integrates seamlessly with any finance ERP (API flexibility, ERP-agnostic, out-of-box SAP HANA connector mentioned on call). (2) Position Zycus as best-of-breed procurement vs bundled ERP procurement modules (Merlin intake adoption advantage, AI depth, composable modules). (3) Accelerate the demo and capability map exchange (action item from 21 May call) to lock Zycus as the procurement choice before the finance ERP decision closes. (4) Leverage the partner (Joel, supply chain consulting) to advocate for best-of-breed procurement vs ERP bundling.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and well-received. Zycus led the 21 May 2026 discovery call with Merlin intake (Teams/Slack bot, zero training, policy-driven), AI-driven touchless invoicing, and dynamic OCR, and the buyer expressed clear interest. Jason asked 'about Dynamic Optical Recognition for handling variable invoice formats, signaling interest in AI-driven invoice processing' (AI excitement signal note, 21 May). Guus acknowledged 'AI-driven touchless invoicing benefits but cautioned on prerequisite of accurate PO data' (AI excitement signal note, 21 May), indicating he understands the value but is focused on data quality and change management prerequisites (a pragmatic, not resistant, stance). Koen's top requirement is 'policy driven guides lines of guidance' (21 May transcript), which maps directly to Merlin intake's governance and zero-training adoption value prop. No AI skepticism or budget pushback expressed. Moore is a procurement-light org (120 trained buyers for 2300 employees, €80M spend, 5000+ suppliers, decentralized across 72 locations) where AI-driven automation and intuitive intake are massive ROI levers, and the buyer sees it. AI is not under-positioned; it is the core wedge. Score: 8/10 (strong buyer interest, clear use case fit, no objections). Not under-positioned.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Moore's stated requirements are well aligned with Zycus capabilities, with one gap (expense management via partnership, not native) and one unresolved concern (cloud-only vs on-prem/isolation for security/geopolitical reasons). Strong fit on: (1) expense + procurement unification (via Webexpenses and Numensia partnerships), (2) multi-language (English/French/Dutch native), (3) Teams/Slack interface (Merlin intake), (4) EU data residency (Germany/Ireland hosting), (5) AI touchless invoicing and dynamic OCR, (6) API/ERP integration flexibility. Partial fit on: (7) on-prem deployment (Zycus cloud-only; buyer raised concern 21 May, Caroline to send technical data protection documentation). Exposed on: none identified yet, but the on-prem concern could escalate if Moore's InfoSec or geopolitical risk assessment rejects cloud-only.

deltas.subject: Buyer articulated significant change management challenge. Guus stated 'ensuring all foundational elements are in place has become a significant challenge in change management' (21 May pain point note). Koen described 2300 decentralized employees across 72 locations with only 120 trained buyers, no system for expense management, and manual AFAS processes. Risk: even if Zycus wins the technical and commercial evaluation, implementation may stall or fail if Moore underestimates the change management effort (data cleanup, policy design, user training, stakeholder buy-in) required to operationalize the system. Zycus does not offer change management services (Caroline: 'we don't do change management', 21 May transcript); Moore will need the partner or internal resources to execute, and if that is not scoped or budgeted, the deal may delay or the system may under-deliver post-go-live.

value.how_we_win: Contractify is a legal-only contract management point solution, not a full S2P platform. Buyer stated it is too complex for procurement use and underutilized. Win by positioning Zycus iContract as a simpler, procurement-native contract module within the full S2P suite, with intake via Merlin in Teams (zero training) and integration to eProcurement and eInvoicing. Contractify stays for legal contracts; Zycus owns procurement contracts.

value.name: Do-nothing / delay (finance ERP bundling risk)
value.quote: We are now deciding what kind of finance ERP we will do, and that's why Guus is checking all the capabilities because we strongly are aware that there is no good finance tool that is also a good procurement tool. So it will be a combination.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 21 May 2026, speaker Koen Bergs (CPO)
value.how_we_win: Moore is running parallel finance ERP and procurement system selections. Risk: the finance ERP vendor (SAP, Oracle, Workday, etc.) bundles procurement and Moore consolidates to a single platform to avoid dual-vendor complexity, or Moore delays procurement decision until finance ERP is chosen. Mitigation: (1) Prove Zycus integrates seamlessly with any finance ERP (API flexibility, ERP-agnostic, out-of-box SAP HANA connector mentioned on call). (2) Position Zycus as best-of-breed procurement vs bundled ERP procurement modules (Merlin intake adoption advantage, AI depth, composable modules). (3) Accelerate the demo and capability map exchange (action item from 21 May call) to lock Zycus as the procurement choice before the finance ERP decision closes. (4) Leverage the partner (Joel, supply chain consulting) to advocate for best-of-breed procurement vs ERP bundling.

value.detail: Buyer articulated significant change management challenge. Guus stated 'ensuring all foundational elements are in place has become a significant challenge in change management' (21 May pain point note). Koen described 2300 decentralized employees across 72 locations with only 120 trained buyers, no system for expense management, and manual AFAS processes. Risk: even if Zycus wins the technical and commercial evaluation, implementation may stall or fail if Moore underestimates the change management effort (data cleanup, policy design, user training, stakeholder buy-in) required to operationalize the system. Zycus does not offer change management services (Caroline: 'we don't do change management', 21 May transcript); Moore will need the partner or internal resources to execute, and if that is not scoped or budgeted, the deal may delay or the system may under-deliver post-go-live.
